wallet_description: Install {link} if you don't have one yet, and copy/paste your address here.
leaderboard_balance_tooltip: Includes GBYTE converted to COOP at the ceiling price
profile_not_found_description: This address has no COOP account.
referral_link_caption: Share this link — when an invitee signs up via your link and makes their first deposit, both of you get a {fixedReward} COOP bonus added to your locked balance. You also earn {coopPct}% from their COOP deposits and {bytesPct}% from their GBYTE deposits as liquid COOP.
withdraw_description: Your lock period has ended. Withdraw your entire balance back to your wallet.
withdraw_unclaimed_warning: You have unclaimed {amount} {symbol} in liquid rewards, please claim them before withdrawing, otherwise the rewards will be lost.
profile_eligible_tooltip: Eligible to vote and receive distributions
profile_ineligible_short_lock: {name} can't vote: unlock date must be at least 1 year in the future
profile_ineligible_both: {name} can't vote: no locked balance and unlock date is too soon
profile_ineligible_no_balance_self: You can't vote: no locked balance
profile_ineligible_short_lock_self: You can't vote: unlock date must be at least 1 year in the future
profile_ineligible_both_self: You can't vote: no locked balance and unlock date is too soon
contributions_description: Recent posts by {user} on {#link to=$forumUrl}Obyte Discord Forum{/link}
contributions_empty_description: Tell the community about your contributions and get votes
vote_disabled_no_balance: {name} has no locked balance — cannot vote for them
vote_disabled_short_lock: {name}'s unlock date must be at least 1 year in the future — cannot vote for them
vote_disabled_both: {name} has no locked balance and unlock date is too soon — cannot vote for them
vote_self: Self-vote is automatic when you vote for others
replace_description: Lock more COOP and unlock an equivalent amount of GBYTE at the formula price.
replace_error_no_bytes: Not enough locked GBYTE to cover this amount
deposit_error_unlock_date: Unlock date must be between 1 and 10 years from now
deposit_desc_attest: Before locking, you must be attested on {telegram} and/or {discord}.
deposit_desc_real_name: If you lock less than {minBalance}, you must be {realName}. This measure helps prevent multiple accounts by the same user.
deposit_banner_title: You are not yet eligible for voting and distributions
deposit_banner_short_lock: Extend your lock period to at least 1 year to become eligible.
deposit_banner_both: Lock tokens for at least 1 year to become eligible.
how_it_works_lock_desc: Lock COOP or GBYTE for 1+ year to earn voting power and emission share.
how_it_works_emission_desc: New COOP is minted daily and distributed in proportion to votes received. Locked rewards grow your balance, liquid rewards are claimable.
how_it_works_vote_desc: Vote for useful contributors. Bigger lock — stronger vote. You earn when others vote for you. Votes expire in 3 months.
hero_title: Earn rewards for {contributing} to the community
hero_subtitle: Lock tokens, vote for contributors, contribute, and receive daily emission rewards. All governed by the community looking to help {#link}Obyte{/link} gain recognition as the most censorship-resistant crypto.
governance_connect_wallet_intro: {wallet} to see your voting power and vote on parameter changes.
governance_profile_balance_tooltip: Your total locked balance used to calculate voting power and emission rewards.
governance_profile_voting_power_tooltip: Square root of your locked balance. Determines the weight of your governance votes.
governance_desc_daily_locked_reward: Fraction of total locked balance minted daily and added to locked balances.
governance_desc_daily_liquid_reward: Fraction of total locked balance minted daily and distributed as liquid COOP.
governance_desc_bytes_reducer: Discount factor applied when converting GBYTE balance to COOP equivalent for emission calculations.
governance_desc_by_votes_share: Share of emission distributed proportionally to votes received (vs. votes × balance).
governance_desc_referrer_coop_deposit_re: Share of COOP deposits credited to the referrer as a reward.
governance_desc_referrer_bytes_deposit_r: Share of GBYTE deposits credited to the referrer as a reward.
governance_desc_referral_reward: Fixed COOP reward for referrer and referee on first deposit.
governance_desc_min_balance_instead_of_r: Minimum COOP balance that exempts a user from real-name attestation.
governance_desc_messaging_attestors: Colon-separated list of attestor addresses for Telegram/Discord verification.
governance_desc_real_name_attestors: Colon-separated list of attestor addresses for real-name verification.
qr_tooltip: Send the transaction from your mobile phone
qr_dialog_title: Scan this QR code with your mobile phone
qr_install_prompt: Install Obyte wallet for {ios} or {android} if you don't have one yet
wallet_popover_tooltip: This will open your Obyte wallet installed on this device to complete the transaction.
faq_what_is_coop_a: Coop is a cooperative community of contributors who help {#link to=|https://obyte.org|}Obyte{/link} grow. All kinds of technical, financial, and social contributions are recognized by fellow community members by voting for contributors, then daily emissions of COOP token are distributed depending on votes.
faq_purpose_a: The purpose is to recognize those who contribute to developing and spreading the word about {#link to=|https://obyte.org|}Obyte’s uncensorable, unstoppable, truly decentralized network{/link}, and encourage new contributions.
faq_how_to_start_a:
Deposit and lock some {symbol} or GBYTE first. The locked amount determines the weight of your votes and one of the two parts of your rewards.

Your funds are locked for at least 1 year. You must link your Obyte address to your {#link to=$telegramBot}Telegram{/link} and/or {#link to=$discordBot}Discord{/link} accounts, so that other community members can know it when they see you in public discussions, and vote for you. If you deposit less than {minBalance} {symbol}, you must be {#link to=$realNameBot}real-name attested{/link} to ensure one man, one account rule.

Then, get engaged in our {#link to=$telegramCommunity}Telegram{/link} and {#link to=$discordCommunity}Discord{/link} communities to learn who is contributing. Vote for them.

Think how you can contribute yourself, and make sure the community knows about your contribution. If they find it valuable, they'll vote for you.
faq_contribution_types_q: What are the types of contributions?
faq_contribution_types_a: There are three main types:{#ol}{#li}{#b}Work{/b} — development (tools, apps, improvements) and social (articles, tweets, videos).{/li}{#li}{#b}Financial{/b} — buying and locking {symbol}/GBYTE in significant amounts.{/li}{#li}{#b}Curation{/b} — recognizing contributors, encouraging votes, discovering underappreciated work, and supporting healthy discussions.{/li}{/ol}
faq_voting_a: Users cast votes for others who make useful contributions.{#ul}{#li}Vote weight equals the square root of the voter's locked balance — this reduces the power of whales and makes smaller accounts matter more compared with the traditional balance-based weight. The system is a middle ground between democracy and plutocracy.{/li}{#li}Vote strength can be 1, 2, or 3 (use 0 to unvote).{/li}{#li}By voting for someone else, you are simultaneously voting for yourself with strength 3. No need to separately vote for yourself.{/li}{#li}Votes expire after 3 months, so contributors must keep contributing to maintain their income.{/li}{/ul}
faq_voter_eligibility_q: Who is eligible to vote and receive distributions?
faq_voter_eligibility_a: Your unlock date must be at least 1 year into the future. This ensures that participants have long-term commitment to the community.
faq_tit_for_tat_voting_a: It's not prohibited. However, it requires separate negotiation for each arrangement and doesn't scale as well as posting about genuine contributions and receiving many votes in response. It resembles the {#link to=|https://friends.obyte.org/|}Friend token{/link} mechanics and encourages pairwise connections, which are useful too.
faq_liquid_rewards_a: They are added to your unlocked balance, and you can claim these rewards at any time. While claiming, you can re-lock all or a portion of them to increase your future rewards (the part that is proportional to your locked balance).
faq_lock_other_tokens_q: Can I lock other tokens besides {symbol}?
faq_lock_other_tokens_a:
Yes, you can lock GBYTE instead. You can choose GBYTE if you are concerned about volatility of {symbol} price or if there isn't enough {symbol} for sale in the market.

However, GBYTE deposits are counted at {bytesReducerPct}% of their value (to encourage deposits in {symbol}).

To calculate the {symbol} equivalent of your GBYTE balance, we use a ceiling price of {symbol} in GBYTE, which follows a formula that starts at 1 and doubles every year. If the market price of {symbol} grows faster than the formula, it becomes more profitable to lock GBYTE.

You can later replace your locked GBYTE with {symbol} at the formula price — to get your GBYTE back, or if the market price of {symbol} falls below the ceiling price.
faq_deposit_amount_a: As much as you like. Since one part of the rewards is proportional to your locked balance, the more you deposit, the more you are rewarded.
faq_unlock_tokens_q: How do I get my locked tokens back, as well as locked rewards?
faq_unlock_tokens_a:
You need to wait until the locking period expires, and then you can freely withdraw the deposited tokens and the accrued locked rewards.

To continue voting and receiving votes, you need to keep the unlock date at least 1 year in the future. If you decide to withdraw, you have to stop extending the unlock date and stop voting for 1 year, and then you can withdraw. The votes you have already received will expire in 90 days, and you'll stop receiving locked and liquid rewards.

Note that if you stop receiving votes (and rewards) while other users continue doing so, your balance will stop growing while their balances will grow 37.8 times by the time you will be able to withdraw yours. The rewards paid daily will increase accordingly. This limits the impact of the withdrawn funds on the market.
faq_prerequisites_q: What are the prerequisites for participating in Coop?
faq_prerequisites_a:
Before depositing funds you need to be attested on {#link to=$telegramBot}Telegram{/link} and/or {#link to=$discordBot}Discord{/link}. The attestations link your Obyte address to your Telegram and Discord usernames, allowing other community members to link your identities in Obyte and the messaging platforms (where the contributions are discussed).

If you deposit less than {minBalance} {symbol} (or equivalent in GBYTE), you also need to be {#link to=$realNameBot}real-name attested{/link}. You don't need to disclose your real name to anyone (except the verification service), but this requirement helps to ensure that the system is not abused by creating multiple accounts belonging to the same person and inflating that person's voting power.
faq_security_and_rules_q: How can I be sure the rules are actually what's described here, can't be changed, and my money can't be stolen?
faq_security_and_rules_a: The rules are implemented by {#link to=|https://obyte.org/platform/autonomous-agents|}Autonomous Agents (AAs){/link}, which are soulless code-driven actors on {#link to=|https://obyte.org/|}Obyte DAG{/link}. Nobody can intervene in their operation, nobody can tell them what to do, nobody can take their money, and nobody can change their code. Even the Obyte team, which created them. Their code is {#link to=|https://github.com/byteball/friend-aa|}available on github{/link}, so anyone can see what rules the agents actually follow and compare with what we describe here.
faq_initial_supply_a: The initial supply is 0. There are no presales or allocations to anyone.
faq_token_minting_a: {symbol} is minted only for rewards to contributors. Most of them are locked and don't create immediate sell pressure.
faq_price_protection_a:
Most of the rewards ({dailyLockedPct}% of the existing supply per day) are paid to locked accounts. Before withdrawing them, the recipients have to wait for 1 year without receiving any rewards for most of this time (after the earlier votes expire). During the same period, locked balances of the remaining users grow {dailyLockedPct}% per day, increasing {lockedGrowthMultiplier} times during the year. When the balance is withdrawn after 1 year, it will be relatively small compared with remaining balances and daily emissions.

The liquid rewards ({dailyLiquidPct}% per day) can be sold immediately but this sell pressure is partially or fully offset by demand for {symbol} from new users who want to lock the tokens and old users who want to lock more.
faq_inflation_a:
Yes, its supply grows due to rewards paid to contributors. However, most of the new emissions go to locked balances and don't immediately affect the market, and the emissions of liquid {symbol} are designed to be counteracted by demand for {symbol} from new users and existing users re-locking (see the previous answer).

The locked balances will eventually be released; however, due to the 1-year delay, they are unlikely to significantly affect the market, as described in the previous answer.
faq_referrals_q: Can I earn by referring users?
faq_referrals_a:
Yes. You also earn when you are referred by someone.

When making your first deposit by following a referrer link, you both get a fixed reward of {referralReward} {symbol} (capped by your balances) to your locked accounts.

The referrer also receives {referrerCoopPct}% of the new user's lifetime deposits ({referrerBytesPct}% of deposits in GBYTE). The reward is paid in liquid {symbol}. Referrers can waive this reward for family and friends.

You can get your referrer link on your profile page for sharing it with potential referrals.
faq_governance_a:
Almost all parameters are changeable by {#link to=|/governance|}governance{/link} vote. In particular, daily rewards percentages and how they are split can be changed. Only users with an unlock date more than 1 year into the future can vote in governance. Governance uses quadratic voting (vote weight = square root of locked balance), reducing the power of whales.

Anyone can suggest changes in any of the governable parameters of Obyte Coop. A bot watches for such suggestions and automatically sends notifications to {#link to=$discordCommunity}Obyte Discord{/link}. Other community members learn about the suggestions from these notifications and can:{#ul}{#li}vote for them to support the proposed changes;{/li}{#li}vote for some other value to oppose the changes;{/li}{#li}do nothing and let others decide.{/li}{/ul}A proposal that has the highest amount of votes and stays unchallenged for 3 days can be committed to make the new parameter value active.

The voting is resistant to manipulation as the requirement of real-name attestation makes it impossible to split one's balance into many smaller ones to gain more voting weight.
faq_team_q: What's the role of the Obyte team?
faq_team_a:
The {#link to=|https://obyte.org|}Obyte{/link} team has developed the {#link to=|https://github.com/byteball/coop-aa|}Coop AA{/link}, which is what handles user balances, deposits, withdrawals, rewards, and the governance framework. After that, the team doesn't operate the AA — it's an autonomous agent, and the team has no power over it. Neither can it change the AA — it's set in stone and nothing can be changed except a few parameters manageable by the community governance.

The Obyte team has also developed and continues to support all the infrastructure around the AA, such as the user interface and integrations with messaging platforms. Independent developers are welcome to contribute to these parts of the ecosystem and get their contribution rewarded in Coop.
faq_why_participate_a: Motivations include: making money through emission rewards, belonging to a community of builders, having the power to recognize and reward others, being appreciated for your contributions, and acquiring a leadership role by influencing votes.
